text: User: I want you to make a script that runs the mcp server in a way that it doesn't inherit any agent session from your session right now and that it captures all http traffic and whatnot so that you can read the logs -- I'm going to connect the ngrok you give me to chatgpt and grok websites and they'll use mcp -- I want you to see everything as is coming, http headers etc so we can debug things [...] Assistant: Everything works end to end: server is fully detached (`ppid=1`), no `TENEX_EDGE_*` identity leaked in, and the access log captures method/tool/headers/clientInfo per request. [...] **Why it's isolated:** this session (`@developer`) had `TENEX_EDGE_AGENT_FALLBACK=developer` in its env, and the MCP `who` tool's auto-provision fallback (the `chatgpt/echo123`-style identity you had built earlier today) only kicks in when *no* anchor resolves. The launched server runs with a scrubbed env (`env -i` + only `PATH/HOME/USER/TMPDIR/LANG`) and is fully reparented to `launchd` (verified `ppid=1`), so its 16-hop ancestor `pty_search` walk can't find "claude" and misattribute every ChatGPT/Grok request to your dev session.
